title: How Wireless Systems Actually Work: Introduction to the Series
summary: An introduction to a six-part series revealing the fundamental physical and information-theoretic constraints that govern all wireless systems. Written to develop engineering judgment by understanding constraints that technology must operate within.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Emergency Locator Transmitters: When Reliability Means Survival
summary: Exploring the Cospas-Sarsat system and how emergency locator transmitters work in real-world conditions where physics, geometry, and reliability constraints intersect.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Doppler, Time, and Geometry in RF: How Motion Encodes Information
summary: Understanding how motion and spatial relationships encode information in wireless systems. Explores how Doppler shift, timing precision, and geometric relationships are fundamental to RF communication.
author: Anil Joseph

title: GNSS: Why Positioning Is Harder Than It Looks
summary: A deep dive into why precision GPS positioning depends on geometry, timing accuracy, and signal strength. Reveals the challenges beyond simple trilateration.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Link Budgets in the Real World: Energy as the Fundamental Constraint
summary: Why energy relative to noise determines detectability in communication systems. A practical guide to understanding link budgets beyond textbook formulas.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Bursty vs Continuous Transmission: How Scheduling Energy Changes Everything
summary: Exploring how the temporal structure of transmission affects detectability, interference, and system performance in RF systems.
author: Anil Joseph

title: AI at the Physical Layer: Where Learning Helps and Where Physics Dominates
summary: Understanding the intersection of machine learning and wireless physical layer. Distinguishing what AI can optimize versus fundamental physical constraints that cannot be learned away.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Logarithmic Thinking and the Decibel: From Sound to Satellites
summary: Why logarithmic scales and the decibel are fundamental to understanding dynamic range in systems from audio to wireless communications. A guide to thinking in orders of magnitude.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Shannon Capacity: The Absolute Limit of Communication
summary: Understanding Claude Shannon's fundamental theorem that defines the maximum rate at which information can be transmitted over a communication channel. The physical and information-theoretic boundaries that no technology can exceed.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Why Shorter Wavelength Does Not Always Mean Shorter Range
summary: Debunking the common misconception about wavelength and propagation range. Exploring how frequency, power, antenna characteristics, and environmental factors actually determine wireless range.
author: Anil Joseph

title: Reading the Indian Power Grid from the Streets: Seeing Engineering in Plain Sight
summary: A visual guide to understanding the power grid by observing street-level infrastructure. Learn to decode voltage levels, insulator designs, conductor bundling, and tower structures that reveal the physics of electrical transmission.
author: Anil Joseph

title: The Sun: Earth's External Power Supply
summary: Understanding the Sun not just as a source of light, but as Earth's primary energy source that drives weather, climate, photosynthesis, and ultimately powers our civilization through solar energy.
author: Anil Joseph
